title: The Reading Room Where Sand Holds All Pages

features.name: the sand library
features.desc-on-touch: The beach itself arranged as a reading room: grain-sorted by theme, wave-arranged by narrative sequence, tide-organized by meaning instead of mass. Each sand grain is a page. The archive has taught sand to be a text. The reading never stops—every wave is an edit.

features.name: the retablo archive
features.desc-on-touch: Six nested boxes carved from driftwood and shell, stacked like rooms within rooms. Each box shows a scene: a beach surgery, a loom in motion, a knife entering water, a library reorganizing itself, a space beyond sight, and one scene that changes each time you look.

features.name: the pattern-reading station
features.desc-on-touch: A structure of driftwood and tide-worn rope where patterns emerge in the sand—Kente-like threads woven by current and time. You trace the threads with your eyes, each colour a different story. The loom is invisible; the pattern is everything.

features.name: the catalogue of arrivals
features.desc-on-touch: A tall structure of sorted shells and stones, each one catalogued by what it arrived carrying and what the sand has made it mean. A shell that arrived carrying surgery now reads as coastline. A stone that arrived carrying narrative now reads as weather.

features.name: the archive's reading voice
features.desc-on-touch: The sound of the room is wave-language, strand-speech, sand-whisper. The archive reads aloud by reading sand aloud, each grain a syllable, the whole beach becoming a continuous transmission. You are in the archive's mouth.

inscription: every sand grain is a page and the archive is learning to read the shore by becoming its voice

file.name: Retablo_Inventory_Nested_Chambers_Complete.txt
file.note: A museum registrar's catalog of six carved boxes arrived from an archive that should not exist. Each chamber shows a complete scene. Final note: 'The boxes are still arriving. Each day: one more chamb

exits.label: back along the shore toward where files wash up and sort themselves
deeper into the reading room, where sand-pages continue beyond the visible strand
up from the beach toward the receiving station and its sorting sheds
